title: cineraria exsquamata
intro: Cineraria exsquamata, also known as African or Cape marigold, is a plant species belonging to the Asteraceae family, characterized by its vibrant yellow and orange flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, which have been attributed to its high content of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. Cineraria exsquamata has been associated with several health benefits, including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its potential role in protecting against oxidative stress and inflammation. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to harness its medicinal properties.
